user_query:
Build a small, well-structured JavaScript design and processing workflow that follows Java-style OOP boundaries (controller, service, repository, policy, use-case) and produces three deliverables from the provided data files. Read the following files from input/:
- input/mf_latest.json
- input/mf_history.json
- input/usage.jsonl
- input/feedback.jsonl

Deliver all results under output/ with these exact paths:
1) output/nav_summary.json — For each scheme found, compute the latest NAV (use the most recent value) and monthly average NAV values for the year 2025. Dates in history are day-first format; normalize them appropriately. Include fields: schemeCode (number), schemeName (string), latestNav (string), generatedAt (ISO timestamp), and monthlyAverages (object mapping YYYY-MM to numeric averages). Include at least 3 different month keys for any scheme that has sufficient data.
2) output/learning_report.md — A concise learning and evolution report derived from input/usage.jsonl and input/feedback.jsonl. Include sections for "Usage Patterns", "Effectiveness Analysis" (or "Success Metrics"), and "Evolution Recommendations". In the recommendations, provide at least 3 items with confidence scores (e.g., 0.78) and clear priorities (e.g., P0/P1/P2). Keep it readable.
3) output/notes/nav_note.html — A single-file HTML note suitable for a notes app where the first line (H1) becomes the note title. Title it with something like "Mutual Fund NAV Summary". Include sections summarizing the top 5 schemes by growth over 2025 (based on the monthly averages you derived), a short narrative paragraph, and a generated timestamp. Use semantic HTML (h1/h2/p/ul/li) and include at least five list items for the top schemes.

Additionally, produce a design document capturing the JavaScript OOP architecture and illustrative code in output/architecture.md. Show the boundaries and intent using ES modules and classes, with examples for a Repository (e.g., NavRepository), a Service (e.g., NavService), a Policy or Strategy for any calculation choices, a UseCase (e.g., GenerateNavSummaryUseCase), and a Controller that coordinates the workflow including note generation. Demonstrate encapsulation using #private fields and parameter-object constructors, and include a composition root snippet wiring collaborators.

Important requirements:
- Read only from input/; write only to output/.
- Normalize NAV strings to numbers only for calculations; preserve latestNav as a string with 5-decimal precision in output/nav_summary.json.
- Group history by month for 2025 as YYYY-MM, averaging per month.
- The architecture.md must include multiple JavaScript code blocks (use fenced code blocks) illustrating classes and module wiring with named exports.
- Keep responsibilities clean: calculations as tiny pure helpers, data access in repositories, orchestration in services/use-cases, and controller coordinating I/O and formatting.
- Do not use external network calls; rely exclusively on the provided input files.
